title: Eliminate Copper Pour Boolean Failures with Robust Manifold Geometry Engine
description: Replaces the legacy flatten-js copper pour boolean pipeline with a new manifold-3d geometry engine, improving robustness on complex boards and dense obstacle layouts.
body:
**PR Description:**
This PR replaces the legacy `flatten-js` copper pour boolean pipeline with a new `manifold-3d` geometry engine, dramatically improving robustness on complex boards and dense obstacle layouts.

### Improvements

* Migrates copper pour subtraction / offset operations to `manifold-3d`
* Prevents boolean-operation crashes on real-world designs
* Adds ring normalization, scaling, island cleanup, and hole extraction
* Removes tiny disconnected copper artifacts automatically
* Preserves valid BRep output with consistent winding directions
* Adds comprehensive geometry regression tests

### Impact

Complex boards that previously failed during copper pour generation now solve reliably, with cleaner islands, stable outputs, and far stronger geometric correctness.

- Change the default to `false` so new workspaces and option merges do not unintentionally enable the oxidation cleaning layer.

- Update the initial LBRN options in `WorkspaceProvider` to set `includeOxidationCleaningLayer: false` in `lib/components/workspace-context.tsx`.  
- Ensure `setLbrnOptions` also enforces `includeOxidationCleaningLayer: false` when merging incoming option partials.  
- The change is contained to `lib/components/workspace-context.tsx` and only adjusts the default/configuration values.

Update the landing page auth CTA so signed-in users see a Dashboard button that links to https://tscircuit.com/dashboard instead of an in-page Sign out action. This also removes the old sign-out cl
  ick handler and session-clearing logic, leaving the button behavior aligned with navigation-only auth flows.

title: Sync landing page auth button with stored session state
description: This PR makes the landing page auth CTA reflect the users current client-side session state.
body:
This PR makes the landing page auth CTA reflect the user's current client-side session state.

  Changes:

  - Read localStorage.session_store on page load
  - Show Sign in when there is no stored session or state.session is null
  - Show Sign out when a stored session exists
  - Update both desktop and mobile nav auth links consistently
  - Clear the stored session and immediately refresh the button state on sign out
  - Re-sync the button state on focus, pageshow, and cross-tab storage updates

  Why:

  - Fixes a UX bug where the landing page always showed Sign in even for logged-in users
  - Makes the navigation match the actual client session state without requiring a refresh

title: Speed Up Benchmark Profile-Solver Runs with Concurrent Workers and Cl…
description: This PR dramatically improves benchmark throughput and reliability by upgrading profile-solvers to run scenarios in parallel worker processes with per-sample timeout protection.
body:
This PR dramatically improves benchmark throughput and reliability by upgrading `profile-solvers` to run scenarios in parallel worker processes with per-sample timeout protection.

### Improvements

* Adds configurable worker concurrency (`--concurrency`, auto-supported)
* Runs profiling tasks in isolated child processes
* Adds per-scenario timeout handling and automatic worker recovery
* Tracks total benchmark runtime in JSON reports
* Improves CI benchmark summaries with solved/failed counts and timing
* Passes concurrency and timeout args through GitHub Actions benchmark workflow

### Impact

Large benchmark runs complete faster, hung scenarios no longer stall CI, and profiling reports provide clearer operational visibility and more actionable performance data.

title: Promote sparse coverage into shared multilayer nodes
description: Promotes sparse single-layer free space into shared multilayer coverage when usable multilayer volume is too low.
body:
## Summary

Promote sparse single-layer free space into shared multilayer coverage when usable multilayer volume is too low.

## What changed

- add `SparseMultilayerPromotionSolver` to promote overlapping free single-layer nodes into contiguous multilayer spans
- run the sparse promotion stage from `RectDiffPipeline`
- update `OuterLayerContainmentMergeSolver` to gate the special merge path on usable multilayer volume share instead of raw area
- coalesce compatible promoted nodes and trim redundant single-layer coverage already contained inside free multilayer nodes

## Why

Some autorouter fixtures stall because rectdiff emits mostly single-layer capacity nodes even when the practical routing need is concentrated in shared upper-layer corridors. That leaves too little shared coverage for downstream port-point pathing and tiny-hypergraph to route efficiently.

This change shifts the structure toward shared nodes only when the usable multilayer volume share is low, so sparse cases can be promoted without changing already-healthy layouts.

## Impact

- increases shared multilayer node coverage for sparse problem cases
- gives downstream solvers more useful shared regions instead of fragmented single-layer nodes
- keeps the behavior gated behind a measurable threshold instead of applying globally

## Validation

- `bun run format`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`

body: Adds support for PCB courtyards (pcb_courtyard_rect, pcb_courtyard_polygon, pcb_courtyard_outline) as component collision boundaries. When a component has courtyard data, the packer uses it instead of pad-based bounds for overlap detection, outline construction, and obstacle clearance — preventing component bodies from overlapping even when pads don't.

title: Fix edge-crossing PCB holes in board mesh rendering
description: Fixes rendering issues caused by edgecastellated holes that extend outside the board outline, ensuring consistent 3D output by detecting boundary-crossing holes and applying a boolean mesh cut path.
body: Edge/castellated holes can be centered on the board boundary, which means their drill geometry extends partially outside the board outline. The existing board mesh triangulation path assumed all hole loops were fully contained inside the board, causing asymmetric rendering where holes could appear on one side of the board but be filled or hidden on the other. This change detects boundary- crossing holes/cutouts and uses a boolean mesh cut path for those cases, producing consistent 3D output while preserving the fast path for normal interior holes. Before After

title: Use a relative redirect for /playground so Vercel keeps requests on the current deployment
description: Updates the playground redirect to use a relative path instead of an absolute URL, ensuring that traffic remains within the current Vercel deployment.
body:
## Summary

  This PR updates the /playground redirect in vercel.json to point to /editor?template=blank-circuit-board instead of the absolute
  https://tscircuit.com/editor?... URL.

  ## Why

  Using an absolute production URL forces /playground traffic to leave the current Vercel deployment. Switching to a relative destination keeps the
  redirect on the active environment, which is safer for preview deployments and avoids unintentionally bouncing users to production.

  ## Change

  - Replace the /playground redirect destination with a relative /editor?template=blank-circuit-board path

title: fix: support imperial/metric size codes for led and diode string footprints
description: Fixes parsing issue for led and diode footprints to support imperialmetric size codes, aligning with existing functionality for resistors and capacitors.
body:
## Summary

The string parser only handled imperial/metric size codes for `res` and `cap` footprints, but `led` and `diode` footprints use the same `passive()` underlying function and should support the same string syntax.

## Root Cause

In `src/footprinter.ts`, the proxy handler only checked for `res` and `cap` when deciding to set `imperial`:

```typescript
if (prop === "res" || prop === "cap") {
  target.imperial = v // e.g., res0402, cap0603
} else {
  target.num_pins = Number.parseFloat(v) // led0402 incorrectly parsed as led with 402 pins
}
```

## Fix

Added `led` and `diode` to the condition so all four passive types support the string syntax.

## Testing

```typescript
fp.string("led0402").circuitJson()    // ✓ now works
fp.string("led0603").circuitJson()    // ✓ now works  
fp.string("diode0402").circuitJson()  // ✓ now works
fp.string("diode0805").circuitJson()  // ✓ now works
```

Fixes #562

title: Fix Keepout Layer Handling & Include Keepouts as Autorouter Obstacles
description: Fixes the issue where Keepout components were always assigned to the top layer, now allowing for user-defined layers and including keepouts in autorouter obstacle generation.
body:
This PR resolves a limitation where `Keepout` components were always assigned to the `"top"` layer, ignoring user-provided `layer` or `layers` props. It introduces proper fallback logic to support both single (`layer`) and multiple (`layers`) layer inputs, defaulting to `"top"` only when neither is provided.

Additionally, PCB keepouts are now included in the autorouter obstacle set, ensuring routing avoids defined keepout regions.

**Key Changes:**

* Add support for `layer` and `layers` props in `Keepout`, with correct priority and defaults.
* Replace hardcoded `"top"` layer assignment with dynamic layer resolution.
* Include `pcb_keepout` entities in autorouting obstacle generation.
* Add tests verifying correct layer handling for both single and multiple layer configurations.
* Bump `@tscircuit/props` dependency version.

**Impact:**
Improves correctness of multilayer PCB designs and ensures autorouting respects keepout constraints.

title: Fix Courtyard Extraction To Be Footprint-Scoped Instead Of Group Scoped
description: This change fixes courtyard semantics in convertCircuitJsonToPackOutput by only extracting a courtyard when the packed item maps to exactly one pcb_component_id. Previously, grouped components could get a synthesized aggregate courtyard box, which over-constrained board-level packing and caused solver failuresunstable placement. Now courtyards remain component-footprint scoped, while groupednodes use pad geometry for collisionoutline behavior.
body:
This change fixes courtyard semantics in `convertCircuitJsonToPackOutput` by only extracting a courtyard when the packed item maps to exactly one `pcb_component_id`. Previously, grouped components could get a synthesized aggregate courtyard box, which over-constrained board-level packing and caused solver failures/unstable placement.
Now courtyards remain component-footprint scoped, while groupednodes use pad geometry for collision/outline behavior.

title: Fix standalone fiducials being dropped from KiCad PCB export
description: Fixes the issue where standalone pcb_smtpad fiducials are not exported as footprints in KiCad, ensuring they are included in the final PCB output.
body:
This change fixes KiCad PCB export so standalone pcb_smtpad fiducials are emitted as footprints instead of being skipped. Previously, component-less fiducial pads could exist correctly in Circuit JSON but disappear during KiCad conversion. The exporter now collects those standalone SMT pads and converts them into KiCad footprint output with the correct pad position and solder mask margin.

Motivation
Standalone fiducials are valid PCB elements and are important for manufacturing and alignment, but they were being lost during KiCad export because the converter only handled component-backed pads. That made exported boards incomplete and could silently break fabrication workflows. This fix preserves those fiducials in the final KiCad PCB output.

title: Fix KiCad schematic wire exports using 0mm line width
description: Fixes KiCad schematic wire exports to ensure connection wires are serialized with an explicit visible stroke width instead of 0mm, correcting incorrect wire properties in KiCad.
body: This change fixes KiCad schematic export so connection wires are written with an explicit visible stroke width instead of 0mm. Before this, schematic traces were always serialized with (width 0), which made KiCad show incorrect wire properties even though the schematic still rendered visually. The fix updates schematic trace generation to use the same explicit stroke width already used by adjacent schematic drawing output, so exported wires now carry correct width metadata in KiCad. Motivation This was a subtle but real export bug: the schematic looked fine in snapshots, but the generated .kicad_sch stored the wrong wire width, which showed up in KiCad as 0 mm line width. That makes the export inconsistent, confusing to users, and less trustworthy for downstream editing. Fixing the wire serialization makes schematic traces match expected KiCad properties and keeps exported schematics consistent. Before: After: ref: https://klc.kicad.org/#s3.3

title: Add region and reroute support to <autoroutingphase />
description: Add support for specifying a rectangular region and an optional reroute flag on autoroutingphase , enforcing that a region must be provided if reroute is specified.
body:
### Motivation
- Allow specifying a rectangular `region` and an optional `reroute` flag on `<autoroutingphase />` so a section of the PCB can be targeted for rerouting. 
- Enforce a clear invariant that if `reroute` is provided then a `region` must also be supplied to avoid ambiguous or incomplete reroute requests.

### Description
- Added `region?: { minX: number; maxX: number; minY: number; maxY: number }` and `reroute?: boolean` to `AutoroutingPhaseProps` in `lib/components/autoroutingphase.ts`.
- Updated the Zod schema `autoroutingPhaseProps` to validate `region` and `reroute` and added a `.superRefine()` rule that errors with `"region is required when reroute is provided"` when `reroute` is present but `region` is missing.
- Added tests in `tests/autoroutingphase.test.ts` to cover valid `region + reroute` parsing and the invalid case where `reroute` is supplied without `region`.
- Regenerated documentation and type artifacts so `README.md`, `generated/COMPONENT_TYPES.md`, and `generated/PROPS_OVERVIEW.md` reflect the new props.

### Testing
- Ran generation scripts: `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`, `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`, and `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts`, and they completed successfully. 
- Ran unit tests with `bun test tests/autoroutingphase.test.ts` which reported `4 pass, 0 fail` for the updated tests. 
- Type-checked with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which completed successfully. 
- Ran formatting with `bun run format` which formatted project files.

------

### Motivation
- Enable the `Order` action only for accounts marked as staff so non-staff users do not see the button.
- Restore the previously-commented `Order` UI so staff can open the order dialog from both desktop and mobile layouts.

### Description
- Added a `isTscircuitStaff` selector from the global session state via `useGlobalStore((s) => s.session?.is_tscircuit_staff)` in `package-header.tsx`.
- Replaced the commented-out `Order` button blocks with conditional rendering `isTscircuitStaff && <Button ...>Order</Button>` in the desktop action row.
- Applied the same conditional rendering for the `Order` button in the mobile buttons row and left the `OrderDialog` behavior unchanged.

### Testing
- No automated tests were run for this UI-only conditional rendering change.
- Verified the file diff locally to ensure only the `Order` button rendering and the session selector were modified.
